name: Polenta with Beef and Sausage Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef into 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Remove casing from the sausage and cut the meat into 1-inch pieces.
3. Chop garlic and parsley together until almost pureed.
4. Cut the mushrooms into thin slices; or use 1/2 oz dried mushrooms and soak them in 1/2 cup lukewarm water for 15 minutes. Drain, saving the water, and chop the mushrooms.
5. Heat olive oil and butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add salt pork; cook until browned.
6. Add onions and saute slowly until medium-brown. Stir in beef and sausage; cook until meat is browned, about 10 minutes.
7. Add garlic, parsley, black pepper, and bay leaf to the pan. Cook for another 10 minutes.
8. Pour in white wine; stir and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
9. Add celery, carrot, tomatoes, and mushrooms; stir and cook until vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes longer.
10. If using dried mushrooms, add their soaking liquid; stir then simmer covered for 40 minutes total.
11. Grate a small amount of nutmeg over the stew and continue to simmer uncovered for an additional 10 minutes.
12. Check beef for doneness; adjust seasoning with salt if needed (salt pork may already provide sufficient flavor).
13. Serve hot polenta alongside the stew, ladling gravy over both.
Instructions followed precisely as written in original recipe.
